thesis: Pre-revenue rare earth mining company developing Tanbreez Project in Greenland — one of the world's largest known rare earth deposits with 4.7 billion-tonne resource and exceptional 27% Heavy Rare Earth Elements content. Strategic JVs with Saudi Arabia ($1.5B) and Romania provide processing capacity and validation. Greenland recently cleared transfer of additional 50.5% stake bringing CRML ownership to 92.5%. Highly speculative with only $7-10M cash and significant funding/execution risks, but positioned to benefit from U.S.-China decoupling and Project Vault tailwinds.

drivers: Saudi JV closes on schedule with full $1.5B commitment
Project Vault provides price guarantees and direct U.S. offtake
Rare earth prices remain elevated ($40+/kg basket)

risks: Saudi JV collapses or significantly delayed
Rare earth prices collapse on Chinese oversupply
Multiple dilutive raises required before production

base_case.assumptions: Saudi JV closes with partial/delayed funding; Project Vault provides indirect support; Production begins 2028; Rare earth prices normalize ($30/kg); One additional equity raise ($100M at $10/share); Successful pilot plant operation.

catalysts.event: Saudi Arabia JV Finalization — $1.5B funding commitment

key_risks: Critical funding risk with only $7.3M cash vs $800M-$1B capex requirement
Greenland regulatory/political risk with independence movement
Heavy insider selling by European Lithium (parent company)
Rare earth price volatility dominated by Chinese supply
High 23.78% short float indicates market skepticism
